title: Fix pull parameter parsing for compat `/build` endpoint
body:
# Changes

Fixing Docker client incompatibility:
```
# Broken compatibility with the docker CLI
docker build . -t alpine-1000 --pull
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.048kB
Error response from daemon: failed to parse query parameter 'pull': "1": invalid pull policy: "1"
```

* Revert https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18583 since it breaks compatibility with standard docker client.
Previously it was believed the `pull` parameter is pull-policy since REST API doc says it is `string`, not `bool`. However it fact it is a boolean. Confusingly enough Docker indeed accept values like `always` or `never` but these values are interpreted as `true` by Docker. As matter of the fact it appears that there is no such a thing as `pull-policy` in Docker.
* Use custom boolean query param deserializer for all compat endpoints to get closer to Docker's way.
This means anything but `""`, `"0"`, `"no"`, `"false"`, `"none"` (ignoring case) is considered to be true.

More context: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17778#issuecomment-1673931925

```release-note
Correct parsing of the pull query parameter for the compat /build endpoint.
```

comments.body: /cc @Luap99 @flouthoc

I do not insist on [the second commit](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19591/commits/61d948c38cee853eb49bb2041130559a5a7e93ac) but we definitely must revert https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18583.
Or if you do not like proliferation of `CompatDecoderKey` we can use that specific decoder only for the endpoint in question (at least for now).
/cc @mheon
wrt `DCO`: do I really need to sign revert commit?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc*, *matejvasek* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Please add rather verbose explanations on _why_ we are reverting the commit.  It has already been released.
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17778#issuecomment-1673931925 sounds reasonable but I agree with @vrothberg, explanations like this should be part of the commit message.
Is there a test to make sure the libpod entry point still works with the string passing?
@rhatdan I don't know, but I think I have not touched libpod part at all.
One questionable thing about this PR is changing boolean parser for all query params of all compat endpoints.

> One questionable thing about this PR is changing boolean parser for all query params of all compat endpoints.

But it changes it in **mostly** compatible way. Strings "true", "1" are still `true`. Strings "false", "0" are still `false`. However string "f" will be now interpreted as `true`, previously it was `false`. But this is how Docker converts string to booleans.
@vrothberg @Luap99 I updated description.
@rhatdan wdyt about https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19591#issuecomment-1674821325 ?

The problem is a lot of the compat endpoint are also used for the libpod endpoints. And I Really do not want such ugly bool parsing for libpod endpoints, sending using `opt=blah` should return a error to the caller and not assume true IMO. A single typo could be fatal otherwise.

Regardless I think for docker endpoints this is reasonable, so I guess if you make sure to guard the decoder step
`decoder := r.Context().Value(api.DecoderKey).(*schema.Decoder)` with `if utils.IsLibpodRequest(r)` and use the correct decoder based on that it should not cause any problem for any libpod endpoints.

>sending using `opt=blah` should return a error to the caller and not assume true IMO

@Luap99  Wholeheartedly agree, but tell that to `moby`.
Also as I said I am fine with just reverting to  previous state where presumably `strconv.ParseBool()` was used.
@Luap99 I fixed decoder discrimination for libpod and compat endpoints.
Draft of little bit more conservative fix https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19601/files

@Luap99 @rhatdan  IMO it would be good to backport this into patch releases (4.6.2). If this PR seems too big/risky for backporting we can consider something more conservative like https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19601/

> @vrothberg @Luap99 I updated description.

I don't see it in the commits.

@matejvasek I think PR description is updated but still it looks less verbose as compared to your investigation here https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17778#issuecomment-1673931925, I'd vote for including this in commit body or alteast a link to it: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17778#issuecomment-1673931925
@vrothberg @flouthoc  I updated commit message.
/lgtm
@rhatdan will this be eligible for 4.6.2?

I think backporting https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19601 makes more sense IMO as this one has less risk involved, if you need the backport in RHEL you need to file a bugzilla as 4.6.1-rhel is already frozen for RHEL.
What will be time period between 4.6.1-rhel and 4.6.2-rhel ? @Luap99

There will never be a 4.6.2 in RHEL, RHEL is frozen for each minor RHEL release. Then only approved backports are allowed to the rhel branches so you need to file bugzillas (or Jira bugs soon) for those in order to get this fixed in RHEL.
4.6.1 will go into RHEL 8.9/9.3 and then next version will be only in 6 months for 8.10/9.4 which by then will likely by podman 4.8 or later whatever we decide upstream.
@Luap99 I absolutely want this fix in rhel.
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19631
@Luap99  https://issues.redhat.com or somewhere else?
@matejvasek I think this is what you're looking for: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2232127
@TomSweeneyRedHat yeah I created that issue after chat on Slack with @Luap99 .
